project.name: AEGIS
project.full_name: Architectural Enforcement and Governance of Intelligent Systems
project.tagline: Capability without constraint is not intelligence.
project.description: An open constitutional governance architecture that enforces deterministic policy at the AI agent action boundary — post-reasoning, pre-execution — making unauthorized actions structurally unavailable.

repos.name: aegis
repos.role: Central authority — governance doctrine, ADRs, schemas, roadmap

repos.name: aegis-constitution
repos.role: Public governance charter, versioned and citeable

repos.name: aegis-governance
repos.role: Technical governance specs: AGP protocol, threat model, federation, ATX-1

repos.name: aegis-core
repos.role: Enforcement engine implementing the governance runtime

repos.name: aegis-sdk
repos.role: Client SDK for TypeScript/JavaScript and Python

repos.name: aegis-federation
repos.role: AT Protocol federation with Lexicon schemas

ieee_computer.title: AEGIS: A Constitutional Governance Architecture for Autonomous AI Agents
ieee_computer.venue: IEEE Computer — Special Issue on AI Governance and Compliance
ieee_computer.author: Kenneth Tannenbaum

key_specifications.AGP-1: AEGIS Governance Protocol — wire protocol for action governance
key_specifications.GFN-1: Governance Federation Network — decentralized governance intelligence sharing
key_specifications.ATX-1: AEGIS Threat Matrix v2.0 — 9 tactics, 25 techniques for agentic AI threat modeling
key_specifications.ACF-1: AEGIS Control Framework — detection, validation, and response for all 25 ATX-1 techniques
key_specifications.ATM-1: AEGIS Adaptive Threat Model — STRIDE-based threat analysis of AEGIS deployments
key_specifications.RFC-0004: Trust architecture — two-layer model (threat detection + reputation)

federation.trust_model: Two-layer: federation publisher trust (GFN-1) + agent runtime trust (RFC-0004)
